nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Corbet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: History: Walter Corbet, a royal knight in 12th-century Scotland, was the first of that name in that kingdom. His 13th-century descendants were major landholders in the Borders, and in the 14th century they acquired lands in the North of Scotland.

variants.name: Corbett
meanings: English (West Midlands, of Norman origin): nickname from Old French corbet ‘raven’, probably denoting someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

History: This is the name of a family descended from Hugh Corbet, a Norman baron who settled in Shropshire following the Norman Conquest. One of his descendants, Sir Richard Corbet, was granted land near Shrewsbury in 1223; since the 13th century, this place has been known as Moreton Corbet. The name was taken from Shropshire to Scotland in the 12th century and to northern Ireland in the 17th century, and thence to North America by at least one group of bearers of the name.

en.lifeSummary: When Catherine de Corbet was born in 1225, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas de Corbet, was 44 and her mother, Isabella de Valletort, was 22. She married William Pipard about 1239, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter.

ko.lifeSummary: Catherine de Corbet(이)가 1225년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas de Corbet(은)는 44세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabella de Valletort(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1239년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 William Pipard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.
